title: I-264 East-West between MM 21.5 and 22.9 Area of US-42 in Jefferson County
description: Construction / Road Work : UPDATE (March 23, 2026) – The Kentucky Transportation Cabinet (KYTC) advises motorists of overnight lane and ramp closures on Interstate 264 East and West (Watterson Expressway) beginning Tuesday, March 24, as part of the $130 million widening and improvement project of I-264 and U.S. 42 (Brownsboro Road) [links-1.govdelivery.com]. Beginning Tuesday, March 24, at 8 p.m., through Wednesday, March 25, at 6 a.m.: Alternating lane closures will occur on I-264 East and West between mile markers 23 and 22 (from the I-264/I-71 split to U.S. 42/Brownsboro Road). Beginning Wednesday, March 25, at 8 p.m., through Thursday, March 26, at 6 a.m.: The I-264 East ramp to I-71 South will be closed. Motorists should continue on I-264 East, take I-71 North, then follow I-265 North to reconnect with I-71 South. Alternating lane closures will occur on I-264 East between mile markers 23 and 22 (from the I-264/I-71 split to U.S. 42/Brownsboro Road). Motorists should heed signage, use caution when traveling through the work zone, and plan ahead for travel delays. The highway improvement project is expected to be completed by the Fall of 2027. More details, including project timelines and traffic impacts [links-1.govdelivery.com], will be shared online as work progresses. Motorists are encouraged to sign up for email or text alerts [links-1.govdelivery.com] regarding weekly traffic impacts pertaining to the project.

title: I-71 North-South between MM 0.1 and 2.0 Between I-64/I-71 Interchange and Zorn Ave. in Jefferson County
description: Construction / Road Work : (March 17, 2026) – Overnight single lane closures are scheduled on Interstate 71 North and South in Louisville for six nights beginning Thursday, March 19, as contract crews perform pavement repairs along the I-71 Widening and Improvement Project [links-1.govdelivery.com] corridor. Traffic is currently shifted to the outside shoulders on both northbound and southbound I-71 to provide crews adequate space to safely prepare for future widening work. Beginning Thursday, March 19, contract crews will remove the existing asphalt and install new pavement in the right lanes between the I-65/I-71 interchange near Frankfort Avenue and the I-71/Zorn Avenue interchange. Overnight closures of the right lane on I-71 North: From 8 p.m. Thursday, March 19, to 5 a.m. Friday, March 20 From 8 p.m. Friday, March 20, to 5 a.m. Saturday, March 21 From 8 p.m. Sunday, March 22, to 5 a.m. Monday, March 23 Overnight closures of the right lane on I-71 South: From 8 p.m. Monday, March 23, to 5 a.m. Tuesday, March 24 From 8 p.m. Tuesday, March 24, to 5 a.m. Wednesday, March 25 From 8 p.m. Wednesday, March 25, to 5 a.m. Thursday, March 26 (March 25, 2026) – The schedule for overnight single lane closures on Interstate 71 North and South in Louisville for contract crews to perform pavement repairs as part of the I-71 Widening and Improvement Project [links-1.govdelivery.com] has been adjusted due to recent weather conditions and fluctuating temperatures. Traffic is currently shifted to the outside shoulders on both northbound and southbound I-71 between the I-65/I-71 interchange near Frankfort Avenue and the I-71/Zorn Avenue interchange to provide crews adequate space to safely prepare for future widening work. Due to recent weather conditions and fluctuating temperatures, four additional nights of overnight single lane closures are expected as crews continue to perform pavement work on I-71. The adjusted schedule is below. Overnight single lane closures on I-71 North: From 8 p.m. Wednesday, March 25, to 5 a.m. Thursday, March 26 From 8 p.m. Sunday, March 29, to 5 a.m. Monday, March 30 Overnight single lane closures on I-71 South: From 8 p.m. Wednesday, March 25, to 5 a.m. Thursday, March 26 From 8 p.m. Thursday, March 26, to 5 a.m. Friday, March 27 From 8 p.m. Friday, March 27, to 5 a.m. Saturday, March 28 From 8 p.m. Sunday, March 29, to 5 a.m. Monday, March 30 These repairs are intended to improve ride quality for motorists. Recent heavy rainfall and fluctuating temperatures caused the asphalt pavement to deteriorate prematurely. All work being performed is part of the $50 million I-71 widening and improvement project. The two-year project will increase capacity for the corridor from four lanes to six lanes, easing traffic congestion, increasing safety, and improving efficiency. The interstate project is expected to be completed by the fall of 2027. The work is being carried out by Haydon Bridge Co. Inc. of Springfield, Kentucky. More details, including project timelines and traffic impacts, will be shared online as work progresses [links-1.govdelivery.com]. Motorists are encouraged to sign up for email alerts regarding traffic impacts pertaining to the project [links-1.govdelivery.com]. The date and duration of this work may be adjusted if inclement weather or other unforeseen delays occur. For the latest traffic and travel updates across the Commonwealth of Kentucky, visit goky.ky.gov [links-1.govdelivery.com]. Information specific to District 5 counties is also available on Facebook [links-1.govdelivery.com] and X [links-1.govdelivery.com].

title: I-65 South between MM 137.0 and 136.7 On Kennedy Bridge in Jefferson County
description: Construction / Road Work : (Feb. 17, 2026) – Work on the John F. Kennedy Memorial Bridge is advancing to the second and final phase of the expansion joint replacement project, which began last year. To assist with the implementation of a traffic shift, rolling roadblocks and lane closures are scheduled to begin Friday, Feb. 20. Contract crews have completed the replacement of the expansion joints in the right three lanes, allowing work to advance to replacing expansion joints in the left three lanes of the bridge. To safely shift traffic and begin the final phase of work, rolling roadblocks and lane closures will take place on Interstate 65 South on the Kennedy Bridge beginning at 7 p.m. on Friday, Feb. 20, continuing through 6 p.m. on Saturday, Feb. 21. During this time, traffic on southbound I-65 will be reduced to the right two lanes to allow contract crews to safely shift all traffic. As part of this transition, the I-65 South to I-64 West ramp, which has been closed to support work on the southernmost expansion joint, will reopen. Following this weekend’s traffic changes, all traffic will be using the right three lanes while work takes place in the left three lanes to replace the remaining expansion joints. This traffic pattern will remain in place through project completion. Overnight closures of the bridge are expected during the final phase of the project. The overnight closures are necessary to prevent any bridge movement while contract crews perform steel repairs underneath the roadway. This work involves precision machining, known as line boring, and the bridge must remain free of traffic to ensure the accuracy and alignment of the repairs. Additional details on upcoming overnight closures will be shared when available. The date and duration of this work may be adjusted if inclement weather or other unforeseen delays occur. For the latest traffic and travel updates across the Commonwealth of Kentucky, visit goky.ky.gov [links-1.govdelivery.com]. Information specific to District 5 counties is also available on Facebook [links-1.govdelivery.com] and X [links-1.govdelivery.com].

title: I-71 North-South between MM 52.7 and 57.3 Between Exit 55 and Exit 62 in Gallatin County
description: Construction / Road Work : A widening project aimed at reducing congestion and improving safety on I-71 in Carroll and Gallatin counties is in progress. The project will widen I-71 from its configuration of two lanes in each direction, to three lanes. Beginning at 7 p.m. on Sunday, April 12, crews will perform ramp work, including milling and paving operations, ramp widening and shoulder work. Due to the scope of work, these ramps will be closed continuously for an extended period during this phase of the project. I-71 southbound off-ramp to Exit 55 (KY 1039) Exit 57 (KY 35/Sparta Pike) on-ramp to I-71 southbound A signed detour will be in place utilizing Boone Road (KY 465). Weather permitting, these ramp closures are expected to wrap up by Sunday, April 26. We will advise if more time is needed to complete the work. Additionally, a single lane closure on I-71 southbound between Exit 55 and Exit 57 will begin at 7 p.m. on Sunday, April 12, to allow crews to adjust the barrier wall. The lane is expected to reopen by 8 a.m. on Monday, April 13.​ *** Traffic on I-71 southbound has shifted onto newly constructed pavement in the median area while crews reconstruct the original southbound lanes. During this phase, crews are removing the existing pavement and completing shoulder work on I-71 southbound. This widening project includes a bridge replacement over KY 47. The current bridges will be replaced with a new, single span bridge matching the median widening throughout the project area. The speed limit in the work zone has been reduced to 55 MPH, and law enforcement will be on-site to ensure compliance. Please, drive carefully throughout the work zone. Weather permitting, the overall project is expected to be completed Nov. 1, 2026. *** A portion of ramp widening work has been completed ahead of schedule. The ramps listed below have reopened: I-71 southbound off-ramp to Exit 55 (KY 1039) Exit 57 (KY 35/Sparta Pike) on-ramp to I-71 southbound​ *** Beginning at 7 p.m. on Sunday, April 26, crews will close Exit 55 (KY 1039) on-ramp to I-71 southbound. During the closure, the contractor will perform ramp work, including milling and paving operations, ramp widening and shoulder work. Due to the scope of work, the ramp will be closed continuously for an extended period during this phase of the project. A signed detour will be in place utilizing I-71 northbound to Exit 57 (KY 35/Sparta Pike) to reenter I-71 southbound. Weather permitting, the ramp closure is expected to be in place through Sunday, May 10. Additional updates will be provided if more time is needed to complete the work. Additionally, a single lane closure on I-71 southbound between Exit 55 and Exit 57 will begin at 7 p.m. on Sunday, April 26, to allow crews to adjust the barrier wall. The lane is expected to reopen by 8 a.m. on Monday, April 27.

title: I-71/75 North Ramp to 12th Street/MLK Jr. Boulevard in Kenton County
description: Construction / Road Work : (June 5, 2026) – Interstate 71/75 ramp closures and detours in Covington are needed to safely advance construction on the Brent Spence Bridge Corridor Project, which will add interstate lanes, better access, a new companion bridge and convert the existing bridge for local travel to improve safety and increase regional highway capacity. Temporary closures and detours will now begin on Monday, June 8, and will take place in multiple phases. On Monday, June 8, the following closures are expected to take place: I-71/75 northbound off-ramp to 12th Street/MLK Jr. Blvd. (0.0 - 0.28 mile points): this closure will remain in place through Summer 2027. Detour: Traffic will use the I-71/75 northbound 5th Street exit ramp, turn left (north) onto Philadelphia Street, then left onto 4th Street driving west to the I-71/75 southbound ramp to Pike Street. Turn left on to Pike Street, then right on to Simon Kenton Way, then left on to MLK Blvd. KYTC will provide further updates ahead of the closures to inform all road users via the Brent Spence Bridge Corridor project website. [links-1.govdelivery.com] Project schedules are subject to change due to inclement weather or other unforeseen circumstances. For the most current information on road construction in Northern Kentucky, please visit the District 6 Roadshow [links-1.govdelivery.com] or the Driving Northern Kentucky Progress [links-1.govdelivery.com] website. You can also stay informed by following KYTC District 6 on Facebook [links-1.govdelivery.com] and X [links-1.govdelivery.com] (formerly Twitter). For statewide traffic and travel updates, visit GoKY [links-1.govdelivery.com].

title: I-64 East Ramp from Hurstbourne Parkway in Jefferson County
description: Construction / Road Work : LOUISVILLE, Ky. (June 16, 2026) – The Kentucky Transportation Cabinet (KYTC) advises motorists of overnight ramp closures on I-64 East tonight, June 16, in Jefferson County. The I-64 East on-ramp from KY 1747 (Hurstbourne Parkway) will be closed from 7 p.m. Tuesday, June 16, through 6 a.m. Wednesday, June 17. The I-64 East off-ramp to KY 1747 (Hurstbourne Parkway) at Exit 15C will also be closed during this time. Contract crews will be performing asphalt resurfacing on portions of the interstate ramps during the overnight closures. The work being performed is part of the current asphalt resurfacing project on KY 1747 (Hurstbourne Parkway) from KY 155 (Taylorsville Road) to KY 1447 (Westport Road), and the project will be substantially complete following tonight's overnight ramp closures. Motorists should heed signage, use caution, and plan ahead for delays.

title: I-71/75 North-South between MM 190.0 and 191.1 Approaching Brent Spence Bridge in Kenton County
description: Construction / Road Work : COVINGTON, Ky. (June 22, 2026) – The Kentucky Transportation Cabinet (KYTC) advises motorists of overnight lane closures on Interstate 71/75 in Kenton County beginning on Monday, June 22. Crews will be filling in rumble strips and performing striping operations ahead of an upcoming traffic shift. Crews will be working on I-71/75 northbound and southbound from south of the 12th Street/MLK Jr. Boulevard off-ramps (190 mile point) to the southern approach of the Brent Spence Bridge (191.1 mile point), a distance of 1.1 miles. Beginning at 8 p.m., crews will block a single left lane in the project area. A double left lane closure will begin at 11 p.m. All lanes are expected to reopen by 5 a.m. daily. All exit ramps currently open will remain open during this portion of the project. Motorists are encouraged to find alternate routes utilizing I-275 and I-471 during working hours to avoid delays. No work will be permitted to take place during the Fourth of July holiday weekend (Friday, July 3 through Sunday, July 5). Weather permitting, this work is expected to be completed by Friday, July 17. KYTC will provide an update once the traffic shift is scheduled.

title: I-275 East between MM 80.7 and 81.2 Between Madison Pike and Turkeyfoot Road in Kenton County
description: Freeway Maintenance : COVINGTON, Ky. (June 23, 2026) – The Kentucky Transportation Cabinet (KYTC) advises motorists of a daytime lane closure on Interstate 275 eastbound in Kenton County beginning on Wednesday, June 24. Crews will be performing pothole repairs on the bridge over Horsebranch Road. Crews will block the right lane on I-275 eastbound between the hours of 9 a.m. and 4 p.m. from east of Turkeyfoot Road (KY 1303) (81.3 mile point) to just past the bridge over Horsebranch Road (80.6 mile point), a distance of 0.7 miles. Both the eastbound I-275 on-ramp from Turkeyfoot Road and I-275 eastbound off-ramp at Madison Pike (KY 17) will remain open during working hours. This work is being performed during daytime hours to provide suitable conditions for the concrete to cure on the bridge deck. Motorists are encouraged to find alternate routes during working hours to avoid any potential delays. Weather permitting, this pothole repair project is expected to be completed on Tuesday, June 30. KYTC will advise if additional days are needed for this repair project. Project schedules are subject to change due to inclement weather or other unforeseen circumstances. For the most current information on road construction in Northern Kentucky, please visit the District 6 Roadshow [links-1.govdelivery.com] or the Driving Northern Kentucky Progress [links-1.govdelivery.com] website. You can also stay informed by following KYTC District 6 on Facebook [links-1.govdelivery.com] and X [links-1.govdelivery.com] (formerly Twitter). For statewide traffic and travel updates, visit GoKY [links-1.govdelivery.com].

title: I-71 South between MM 3.5 and 0.5 Area of Zorn Avenue in Jefferson County
description: Construction / Road Work : LOUISVILLE, Ky. (June 29, 2026) – The Kentucky Transportation Cabinet (KYTC) advises motorists of rolling roadblocks and alternating lane closures on Interstate 71 South today, June 29, as part of the $50 million I-71 widening and improvement project in Louisville [links-1.govdelivery.com]. Rolling roadblocks and alternating lane closures will take place on I-71 South at mile point 0.5 (near Edith Road) from 4 p.m. to 5 p.m. today, June 29. The rolling roadblocks and alternating lane closures are necessary for contract crews to perform pothole patching operations. Traffic will be temporarily slowed and, at times, stopped to allow contract crews space to safely perform work. Motorists should heed rolling roadblock restrictions, maintain safe following distances, and use caution when traveling through the work zone.

title: I-64 East-West between MM 48.0 and 52.0 Between KY 151 and KY 2817 in Franklin County
description: Construction / Road Work : LOUISVILLE, Ky. (June 26, 2026) – The Kentucky Transportation Cabinet (KYTC) advises motorists of alternating lane closures on Interstate 64 East and West beginning Wednesday, July 1, in Franklin County. Alternating lane closures will occur daily from 9 a.m. to 3 p.m. on I-64 East and West between mile markers 48 and 52 (between KY 151 and KY 2817) on the following dates: Wednesday, July 1 Thursday, July 2 Monday, July 6 Tuesday, July 7 The alternating lane closures are necessary for contract crews to perform bridge maintenance. Motorists should heed signage and use caution when traveling through the work zone.

title: I-64 East between MM 53.0 and 58.0 Between U.S. 127 and U.S. 60 in Franklin County
description: Construction / Road Work : The Kentucky Transportation Cabinet (KYTC) advises motorists of overnight alternating lane closures on Interstate 64 East beginning Monday, July 6, in Franklin County. Overnight alternating lane closures will occur nightly from 7 p.m. to 6 a.m. beginning Monday, July 6, through Tuesday, July 21, on I-64 East between mile markers 53 and 58 (between U.S. 127 and U.S. 60). The overnight alternating lane closures are necessary for contract crews to perform asphalt paving operations. Motorists are advised to follow all lane closure restrictions, use caution when traveling through the work zone, and plan for additional travel time. The work is part of the I-64 improvement project [links-1.govdelivery.com] between the Frankfort exits (mile markers 53 and 58), which includes milling removing the existing asphalt surface followed by resurfacing with new asphalt pavement. Other improvements to the 5-mile corridor include bridge repairs and bridge crash wall repairs. Construction is scheduled to be completed by the Fall of 2026.

title: I-264 East-West between MM 15.0 and 21.0 Between Newburg Road and Westport Road in Jefferson County
description: Construction / Road Work : The Kentucky Transportation Cabinet (KYTC) advises motorists of overnight alternating lane closures on Interstate 264 East and West (Watterson Expressway) beginning Tuesday, July 7, in Jefferson County. Overnight alternating lane closures will occur on I-264 East and West (Watterson Expressway) between mile markers 15 and 21 (between KY 1703/Newburg Road and KY 1447/Westport Road) nightly from 9 p.m. to 5 a.m. beginning Tuesday, July 7, through Tuesday, July 21. Additionally, the right lane of the off-ramp from I-264 West to northbound U.S. 31E (Bardstown Road) at Exit 16, will be closed on Monday, July 13, from 9 p.m. to 5 a.m. on Tuesday, July 14. The overnight alternating lane closures are necessary for contract crews to replace overhead signage. Motorists are advised to follow all lane closure restrictions, use caution when traveling through the work zone, and plan for additional travel time.
